methodology: Cross-reference network (9v bidirectional GEN.28.12↔JHN.1.51; 7v 'heaven opened' pattern to Act7:56, Eze1:1, Lk3:21) + patristic reading (10 commentaries on Gn 28:12 with 4 distinct interpretations: Christological, Soteriological/Cross, Ecclesiological, Incarnational) + CCEL (MacLaren 'everywhere the ladder stands', White 'only medium of communication') + topic verification (JACOB, ANGELS, VISIONS, LADDER)

evidence: commentaries (10 entries! 4 distinct interpretations: Aphrahat 'gate+cross', Ambrose 'foresaw Christ', Augustine 'Church as living ladder', Bede 'born on earth lives in heaven', Caesarius 3x 'lean on ladder=hang on cross', Chromatius 'cross with virtue-steps', Cyril 'Christ on top as God', Haydock 'Incarnation')
commentaries (Chrysostom 'angelic chronology: birth→ministry→cross→resurrection→ascension', Cyril 'proof of divine nature', Haydock)

new_refs_added.justification: Aphrahat: 'gate of heaven = Christ' (John 10:7-9). 'How awesome is this place! This is the gate of heaven.' Echoes 'heaven opened' in Jn 1:51. Names Bethel ('house of God'). Completes the vision narrative.

candidates_noted.justification: 6v→GEN.13.15. Cyril: 'Christ on top, as God and Lord.' God standing at the top of the ladder = divine presence.
7v de GEN.28.12 AND JHN.1.51. 'I see heaven opened and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.' Direct echo of Jacob's ladder in Stephen's martyrdom.
Haydock cites in Gn 28:12. JHN.14.6→JHN.10.9 = 66v. 'I am the way' = the ladder personified. 1Tim 2:5 ('one mediator') complements.

four_interpretations.description: 4 distinct patristic interpretations of the ladder coexisting without contradiction

interpretations.name: Christological
interpretations.formula: The ladder IS Christ — the person who bridges heaven and earth. 'The gate of heaven is Christ' (Aphrahat). 'Foresaw Christ on earth' (Ambrose). 'Incarnation of Jesus Christ' (Haydock).

interpretations.name: Soteriological (Cross)
interpretations.formula: The ladder IS the cross. 'Leaning on the ladder = hanging on the cross' (Caesarius). 'Steps of virtue: faith, justice, chastity, holiness, patience, piety' (Chromatius).

interpretations.name: Ecclesiological
interpretations.formula: The ladder IS the Church. 'Angels = good preachers of Christ' (Augustine). 'Born on earth but way of life in heaven' (Bede).

interpretations.name: Incarnational
interpretations.formula: Christ simultaneously at top (heaven) AND bottom (earth). 'No one has ascended to heaven except the Son of Man who IS in heaven' (Jn 3:13).

four_interpretations.significance_for_tcc: A search for 'escada de Jacó' should retrieve Gn 28:12 and Jn 1:51, but the theological depth resides in the commentaries. The 4 interpretations show that the same image can sustain multiple valid readings — this is a test of whether semantic search captures the concept beyond the literal 'ladder' vocabulary.

typological_substitution.description: Jesus replaces the LADDER with HIMSELF
typological_substitution.old: Gn 28:12: 'angels ascending and descending ON IT (the ladder)'
typological_substitution.new: Jn 1:51: 'angels ascending and descending ON THE SON OF MAN'
typological_substitution.significance: The mediator is not a STRUCTURE but a PERSON. Christ does not point to the ladder — He IS the ladder.

jerome_cross_query_link.discovery: Jerome identifies Jacob's STONE (pillow) as Christ the CORNERSTONE (Is 28:16, 1Pe 2:4-8). This creates an unexpected link between query v2_hard_09 (Jacob's ladder) and v2_hard_02 (cornerstone).
jerome_cross_query_link.significance: Cross-query validation: the same narrative (Gn 28) connects to TWO different queries in our set, confirming both are theologically related.

heaven_opened_pattern.significance: Heaven opens in CHRISTOLOGICAL events — the pattern established in Jacob's dream recurs at key moments in salvation history.
